## Choosing the number of slides

You set the slide count before generating. As a rule of thumb:

- **5–7 slides** — a short update, a single idea, a lightning talk.
- **8–12 slides** — a standard meeting presentation with room for context and next steps.
- **13+ slides** — training material or a detailed walkthrough; consider splitting very broad subjects into separate decks instead.

Fewer slides give the AI more room per slide; more slides spread the topic thinner, so broad topics fit longer decks better than narrow ones.

## What the generator produces

Every generated deck is built from three layout types:

1. **Title slide** — a title and subtitle for the deck.
2. **Bullet-point content slides** — the body of the presentation.
3. **Image + text slides** — mixed layouts that pair a visual with supporting text.

All of it consists of native Google Slides objects: you can reword text, move elements, change the theme, and export to PPTX or PDF via the normal **File → Download** menu.

## Example topics with recommended settings

| Topic | Slides | Tone | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| "Q3 marketing results and Q4 priorities for the leadership team" | 8 | Professional | Add your actual numbers via the additional content field |
| "Introduction to composting for a community workshop" | 10 | Friendly | Broad how-to subject, benefits from more slides |
| "Pitch: subscription pricing for our mobile app" | 6 | Persuasive | Short and focused works best for pitches |
| "New employee IT security training" | 12 | Formal | Training content; longer deck, one rule per slide |
| "Nuestro plan de lanzamiento para el mercado español" | 8 | Professional | Set the output language to Spanish to generate the deck natively |

Tone names vary by add-on version — pick the closest option in the sidebar. For tone guidance and the full language workflow, see <a href="/docs/slides-ai/writing-tones-and-languages">Writing tones and languages</a>.


### Create slides from text, notes, or an outline (https://docgpt.ai/docs/slides-ai/create-slides-from-text)

# Create slides from text, notes, or an outline

Besides generating from a topic alone, <a href="/docs/slides-ai">Slides AI by DocGPT</a> has an optional **additional content** field where you paste text the AI should use as source material — meeting notes, a blog post, a report outline. The generator then builds the deck from your content instead of writing everything from scratch.

## How to use the additional content field

1. Open your presentation and go to **Extensions → AI GPT for Slides → Start**.
2. Enter a short topic that names the deck, for example "Weekly product sync — 12 July".
3. Paste your source text into the additional content field.
4. Set the number of slides, tone, and language, then generate.

The topic still matters: it acts as the working title and tells the AI what the deck is for, while the pasted text supplies the substance.

## What the AI does with your text

The generator restructures your source text into the standard deck layouts: a title slide, bullet-point content slides, and image + text slides. In practice that means it:

- groups related points from your text onto the same slide,
- condenses paragraphs into short bullet points,
- orders the slides to follow the flow of your source material.

The output is a starting draft built from your content, not a verbatim copy — review it and adjust wording where precision matters. All slides are regular Google Slides objects, so editing is the same as in any deck.

## Common sources

- **Meeting notes** — paste the raw notes; ask for 6–8 slides to get a recap deck with decisions and action items grouped together.
- **Blog post or article** — paste the full text; one slide per section is a reasonable slide-count estimate.
- **Report outline** — paste the outline with its headings; the headings tend to become slide titles, so clean them up first.

## Tips for clean source text

- **Remove noise first.** Strip signatures, timestamps, and off-topic chatter from notes before pasting.
- **Use headings or blank lines** to separate sections — structure in the input leads to better slide grouping.
- **Match slide count to content volume.** A one-page outline does not fill 15 slides; a long report will not compress well into 5.
- **One document per deck.** Pasting two unrelated documents produces a muddled deck; generate them separately.
- **Mind the language.** If your source text and the selected output language differ, the AI generates in the output language — handy for producing, say, a Spanish deck from English notes. See <a href="/docs/slides-ai/writing-tones-and-languages">Writing tones and languages</a>.

## Related pages

- <a href="/docs/slides-ai/create-presentation-from-topic">Create a presentation from a topic</a> — when you have no source text
- <a href="/docs/slides-ai/credits-and-troubleshooting">Credits and troubleshooting</a> — if generation fails on long inputs


### Writing tones and output languages in Slides AI (https://docgpt.ai/docs/slides-ai/writing-tones-and-languages)

# Writing tones and output languages

Before generating a deck, <a href="/docs/slides-ai">Slides AI by DocGPT</a> lets you set two options that shape the writing: the **tone** and the **output language**. Both are selected in the sidebar under **Extensions → AI GPT for Slides → Start**.

## Choosing a writing tone

The tone controls how the AI phrases the slide text — the facts stay the same, the voice changes. Pick the tone in the sidebar to match the audience and setting:

- **Formal / professional tones** — board decks, client presentations, compliance training. Full sentences on title lines, no colloquialisms.
- **Friendly / casual tones** — internal team updates, community workshops, onboarding decks where approachability matters more than polish.
- **Persuasive tones** — pitches and proposals where the deck argues for a decision rather than just informing.

If you are unsure, a professional tone is the safe default for workplace decks. The tone applies to the whole deck; to change it, regenerate with a different setting or edit individual slides by hand.

## Generating decks in other languages

The output language option supports 40+ languages, including Arabic, Chinese, Hindi, Japanese, Korean, Spanish, French, German, Portuguese (both European and Brazilian), Russian, and Ukrainian.

The output language is independent of your input language: you can type an English topic, or paste English notes into the additional content field, and generate the deck directly in Spanish or Japanese. The whole deck — title slide, bullets, image + text slides — is written in the selected language.

Practical notes:

- **Portuguese comes in two variants** — pick European or Brazilian to match your audience.
- **Text length varies by language.** German or French text runs longer than English; after generating, check that no text overflows its placeholder and shorten where needed. Slides are native Google Slides objects, so editing is straightforward.
- **Right-to-left languages** such as Arabic are supported as output languages; review text alignment after generating.

## Two ways to get a multilingual deck

1. **Generate directly in the target language.** Set the output language before generating. Best when the deck only needs to exist in one language.
2. **Generate in English, then translate.** Build and polish the deck in English, then use the separate <a href="/docs/slides-ai/translate-presentation">Translate Slides™ add-on</a> to translate the finished deck — element by element or all at once — while preserving the layout. Best when you need the same deck in several languages, since you only edit and fact-check one master version.

## Related pages

- <a href="/docs/slides-ai/create-presentation-from-topic">Create a presentation from a topic</a> — example topics with tone settings
- <a href="/docs/slides-ai/translate-presentation">Translate a presentation</a> — the Translate Slides™ add-on how-to


### Translate a presentation with Translate Slides (https://docgpt.ai/docs/slides-ai/translate-presentation)

# Translate a presentation with Translate Slides™

**Translate Slides™ with AI GPT** is a separate sister add-on from DocGPT.ai that translates an existing Google Slides presentation into a target language. Unlike the generator in <a href="/docs/slides-ai">Slides AI by DocGPT</a>, it does not create new slides — it takes the deck you already have and replaces the text with a translation while preserving the layout.

See the <a href="/translate-slides-with-ai-gpt/">Translate Slides landing page</a> for features, pricing, and the install link.

## When to use it

- You built and polished a deck in one language and need the same deck in another.
- You need several language versions of one master deck — translate copies instead of regenerating each one.
- You received a deck in a language your audience does not read.

If the deck does not exist yet, it is usually simpler to generate it directly in the target language — see <a href="/docs/slides-ai/writing-tones-and-languages">Writing tones and languages</a>.

## How to translate a deck

1. Install **Translate Slides™ with AI GPT** — start from the <a href="/translate-slides-with-ai-gpt/">landing page</a> and follow its install link, approving permissions for the account you use with Google Slides.
2. Open the presentation you want to translate. Work on a copy (**File → Make a copy**) if you want to keep the original language version.
3. Launch the add-on from the **Extensions** menu.
4. Choose the scope: translate the **selected slide elements** only, or the **whole deck**.
5. Pick the target language and run the translation.

## What happens to formatting

- The slide layout is preserved: element positions, images, and slide structure stay where they are, and the translated text replaces the original text in place.
- Translated text can be longer or shorter than the original — German typically runs longer than English, for example — so review each slide for text that overflows its placeholder and adjust font size or wording where needed.
- The result is still a normal Google Slides deck: fully editable, exportable to PPTX or PDF via **File → Download**.

## Scope: selected elements vs. whole deck

- **Selected elements** — useful for partially bilingual decks, or for retranslating a single text box you edited after a full translation.
- **Whole deck** — the usual choice for producing a complete language version in one run.

## Related pages

- <a href="/docs/slides-ai/writing-tones-and-languages">Writing tones and languages</a> — generate a deck directly in the target language instead
- <a href="/docs/slides-ai/credits-and-troubleshooting">Credits and troubleshooting</a> — add-on not visible in the Extensions menu and other common issues


### Slides AI credits, upgrades, and troubleshooting (https://docgpt.ai/docs/slides-ai/credits-and-troubleshooting)

# Credits and troubleshooting

This page covers how the free plan of <a href="/docs/slides-ai">Slides AI by DocGPT</a> works, what to do when you run out of credits, and fixes for the most common add-on issues.

## How free credits work

- The free plan includes **10 AI presentations per month**.
- No credit card is required — the free allowance is active as soon as you <a href="https://workspace.google.com/marketplace/app/create_presentation_with_ai_chatgpt_slid/517982554715">install the add-on</a>.
- Each generated presentation counts against the monthly allowance.

## When credits run out

When you use up the monthly allowance, generation pauses until the allowance renews the next month. To keep generating without waiting, upgrade to a paid plan — plans start at $14.99/mo. Current tiers and what they include are listed on the <a href="/google-slide-ai/#pricing">pricing section of the Slides AI page</a>.

## Common issues

### The add-on is not visible in the Extensions menu

1. Refresh the browser tab with your presentation — the Extensions menu is only rebuilt on page load.
2. Confirm you are signed in with the Google account that installed the add-on. Check the account avatar in the top-right corner of Google Slides.
3. If it is still missing, reinstall from the <a href="https://workspace.google.com/marketplace/app/create_presentation_with_ai_chatgpt_slid/517982554715">Marketplace listing</a> while signed in with the correct account.

### Generation fails or never finishes

1. Retry the generation — transient failures usually succeed on a second attempt.
2. Shorten the topic. Very long topics (or very large pasted text in the additional content field) are the most common cause of failed runs; trim the input and try again. See <a href="/docs/slides-ai/create-slides-from-text">Create slides from text</a> for tips on preparing source text.
3. Reduce the slide count and generate again.

### Signed in to multiple Google accounts

Google Slides add-ons can misbehave when several Google accounts are signed in to the same browser session: the add-on may run under a different account than the presentation. Workarounds:

- Open the presentation from your **default account** — the one at `https://docs.google.com/presentation/u/0/`.
- Or sign out of the extra accounts, or use a separate browser profile (or an incognito window) with only the account that installed the add-on.

## Where to get support

If none of the above helps, see the general <a href="/docs/support-gpt-sheets-docs-slides-forms-gmail/">Troubleshooting GPT for Google Sheets, Docs, Slides, Forms, Gmail</a> section, which includes contact options for the DocGPT.ai support team.


### AI Pitch Deck Generator for Google Slides | DocGPT.ai (https://docgpt.ai/ai-pitch-deck-generator/)

## Short answer {#short-answer}

An **AI pitch deck generator** is only useful if the output lands somewhere you can actually work on it. [Slides by DocGPT.ai](/google-slide-ai/) generates a complete Google Slides deck from a topic: you choose the number of slides, the writing tone, and the language (40+ supported), and you can paste your own outline as source content so the AI follows *your* pitch structure instead of inventing one. The result is a native, fully editable Google Slides file — title slide, bullet slides, and image+text layouts — not a locked export from a separate design tool.

The fastest path for a founder: [install the add-on](https://workspace.google.com/marketplace/app/create_presentation_with_ai_chatgpt_slid/517982554715) → open Google Slides → **Extensions → AI GPT for Slides → Start** → enter your topic → set 10 slides → paste the outline below → generate → replace the placeholders with your real numbers. The free plan includes 10 AI presentations a month with no credit card; paid plans start at $14.99/mo — [compare pricing](/google-slide-ai/#pricing).

Slides by DocGPT.ai is part of the DocGPT.ai suite used by 500,000+ users.

## How it works {#how-it-works}

1. **Install the add-on.** Get [Slides by DocGPT.ai from the Google Workspace Marketplace](https://workspace.google.com/marketplace/app/create_presentation_with_ai_chatgpt_slid/517982554715). It runs inside Google Slides, so there is no separate app to learn.
2. **Open the generator.** In any Google Slides file, go to **Extensions → AI GPT for Slides → Start**.
3. **Describe the deck.** Enter a topic, choose the number of slides (10 for a classic investor deck), pick a writing tone (for example, confident and concise), and select a language.
4. **Paste your outline as source content.** This is the step most founders skip. Pasting your own pitch structure and facts into the additional-content field keeps the AI grounded in your company instead of generic startup boilerplate.
5. **Generate and edit.** The deck appears as normal Google Slides. Rearrange slides, rewrite bullets, apply your theme, and comment with cofounders exactly as you would in any deck. Export to PowerPoint or PDF via Google Slides itself (**File → Download**).

For a step-by-step walkthrough, see the docs: [create a presentation from a topic](/docs/slides-ai/create-presentation-from-topic) and [create slides from your own text](/docs/slides-ai/create-slides-from-text).

## Worked example: a 10-slide investor deck {#worked-example}

Settings to use in the add-on:

- **Slides:** 10
- **Tone:** professional and confident
- **Language:** English (or any of 40+)
- **Topic:** `Seed-stage investor pitch deck for Acme Robotics, warehouse automation robots for mid-size 3PL operators`

Then paste this into the source-content field, edited for your company. The bracketed placeholders are deliberate — fill them with real numbers before you generate, or after, in the editable deck:

```
Company: Acme Robotics — plug-and-play warehouse automation robots for
mid-size 3PL operators.

Follow this 10-slide structure:
1. Title — Acme Robotics: warehouse automation for the mid-market.
2. Problem — mid-size warehouses can't afford enterprise automation
   projects, and labor shortages are getting worse.
3. Solution — picking robots that deploy in days, not months, with no
   warehouse redesign.
4. Market — [insert your TAM / SAM / SOM figures and sources].
5. Product — how it works in 3 steps: map the aisle, train the picker,
   go live.
6. Traction — [insert real revenue, pilots, LOIs, growth rate].
7. Business model — robotics-as-a-service subscription per robot
   per month.
8. Competition — enterprise vendors vs. us on cost and deployment speed.
9. Team — [founders' names and the experience that earns the right
   to win].
10. The ask — raising [amount] to reach [specific milestone].
```

Generate, and you get a first draft of all 10 slides in native Google Slides. Because the deck is fully editable, the iteration loop with cofounders happens where it should: rewrite the problem slide, drop in your real traction chart, and re-order slides for the specific investor meeting — no re-generating, no export/import round trips.

<div class="seo-guide-inline-cta">
  <div>
    <strong>Draft your deck tonight, rehearse it tomorrow</strong>
    <span>Install Slides by DocGPT.ai free, paste your outline, and get an editable 10-slide investor deck in minutes.</span>
  </div>
  <a class="seo-guide-btn seo-guide-btn-primary" href="https://workspace.google.com/marketplace/app/create_presentation_with_ai_chatgpt_slid/517982554715">Install Slides by DocGPT.ai</a>
</div>

## Where founders use it

- **First seed deck** — get from blank page to a complete structure in one sitting, then spend your time on the story, not the scaffolding.
- **Demo day variants** — regenerate the same outline at 5 slides for a 3-minute demo-day format, then tighten by hand.
- **Investor update decks** — a monthly update follows a repeatable structure; paste last month's notes as source content.
- **Partnership and BD decks** — reuse the product and traction slides, swap the problem framing per audience.

## What the AI drafts — and what founders must add {#honest-limits}

Be realistic about the division of labor. Slides by DocGPT.ai is genuinely good at the part founders procrastinate on: turning an outline into a complete, coherently structured deck with clean slide copy in your chosen tone. It will not know your real numbers. Market size, revenue, pilot results, team bios, and the size of your ask must come from you — that is why the worked example above uses explicit `[placeholders]` instead of letting the AI improvise figures. Investors notice invented stats, and an AI-generated market-size claim is not diligence-proof. Treat the generated deck as a strong first draft: the structure and copy are done, and your job shifts to plugging in real evidence and rehearsing the narrative.

- [Create slides from your own text](/docs/slides-ai/create-slides-from-text)
- [AI sales deck generator](/ai-sales-deck-generator/)
- [Turn a blog post into a presentation](/blog-post-to-presentation/)

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Can AI generate a full investor pitch deck in Google Slides?

Yes. Slides by DocGPT.ai generates a complete deck — title slide, bullet slides, and image+text layouts — directly in Google Slides. You set the number of slides and tone, and you can paste your own pitch outline as source content so the deck follows the classic 10-slide investor structure. Everything stays natively editable.

### Do I still need to add my own numbers?

Yes. The AI drafts the structure and slide copy; it does not know your traction, market size, or financials. Use placeholders in your outline, then replace them with real, verifiable figures in the editable deck before you send it to an investor.

### How much does an AI pitch deck generator cost?

The free plan includes 10 AI presentations per month with no credit card required — enough to draft and re-draft a seed deck. Paid plans start at $14.99/mo. See [Slides by DocGPT.ai pricing](/google-slide-ai/#pricing).


### AI Sales Deck Generator for Google Slides | DocGPT.ai (https://docgpt.ai/ai-sales-deck-generator/)

## Short answer {#short-answer}

An **AI sales deck generator** earns its place when it does the thing reps skip under time pressure: personalizing the deck for the prospect on the calendar. [Slides by DocGPT.ai](/google-slide-ai/) generates a complete Google Slides presentation from a topic — you choose the number of slides, the writing tone, and the language (40+ supported), and you can paste your own text as source content. For sales, that source-content field is the whole trick: paste your prospect research, and the deck comes back framed around *their* company, not a generic pitch.

The workflow: [install the add-on](https://workspace.google.com/marketplace/app/create_presentation_with_ai_chatgpt_slid/517982554715) → **Extensions → AI GPT for Slides → Start** → topic names the prospect → paste your research notes → pick the tone your brand sells in → generate → tighten the proof and pricing slides by hand. The output is native, fully editable Google Slides, exportable to PowerPoint or PDF via **File → Download**. Free plan: 10 AI presentations a month, no credit card; paid from $14.99/mo — [see pricing](/google-slide-ai/#pricing).

Slides by DocGPT.ai is part of the DocGPT.ai suite used by 500,000+ users.

## How it works {#how-it-works}

1. **Install the add-on** from the [Google Workspace Marketplace](https://workspace.google.com/marketplace/app/create_presentation_with_ai_chatgpt_slid/517982554715).
2. **Open the generator** inside any Google Slides file: **Extensions → AI GPT for Slides → Start**.
3. **Name the deck for the prospect.** A topic like "Discovery-call deck for DataBridge tailored to Northwind Logistics" produces sharper slides than "sales deck".
4. **Paste your research as source content.** Call notes, recent press, job postings, current tooling — whatever you would normally skim five minutes before the call. The AI grounds the deck in what you pasted.
5. **Pick the tone.** Consultative, direct, friendly, formal — choose the writing tone that matches how your brand actually talks to buyers, and keep it consistent across the team's decks.
6. **Generate and edit.** The deck is normal Google Slides: swap in your real customer proof, adjust the pricing slide, apply your brand theme, and duplicate it as the starting point for the next prospect in the segment.

Docs walk through both modes: [create a presentation from a topic](/docs/slides-ai/create-presentation-from-topic) and [create slides from your own text](/docs/slides-ai/create-slides-from-text).

## Worked example: a discovery-call deck from prospect research {#worked-example}

Settings to use in the add-on:

- **Slides:** 8
- **Tone:** consultative
- **Language:** English (or any of 40+)
- **Topic:** `Discovery-call sales deck for DataBridge, a managed ETL platform, tailored to Northwind Logistics`

Paste this into the source-content field, swapping in your own product and the research you actually gathered:

```
Our product: DataBridge — managed ETL pipelines for mid-market
operations teams.

Prospect: Northwind Logistics, ~800 employees, runs SAP plus 14
regional warehouse systems.

Research notes:
- Recent press: opening 3 new distribution centers in 2026.
- Job postings: hiring 2 data engineers "to consolidate warehouse
  reporting".
- Current stack (from the intro call): nightly CSV exports and
  Excel-based KPI reporting.

Follow this 8-slide structure:
1. Title — DataBridge x Northwind Logistics.
2. What we heard — Northwind's goals, in their words.
3. The cost of manual reporting across 14 (soon 17) sites.
4. How DataBridge consolidates warehouse data into one pipeline.
5. What a 30-day rollout looks like.
6. Proof — [insert your real customer outcomes for similar
   logistics teams].
7. How pricing works.
8. Next steps — a scoped pilot on two warehouses.
```

The generated deck opens with the prospect's own situation — their expansion, their hiring signal, their CSV-and-Excel status quo — instead of ten slides about your company. Before the call, you do the human pass: confirm the research is current, drop in a real case study on slide 6, and set the pilot scope on slide 8.

<div class="seo-guide-inline-cta">
  <div>
    <strong>Personalize the next call's deck in minutes</strong>
    <span>Install Slides by DocGPT.ai free, paste your prospect notes, and walk in with a deck about them.</span>
  </div>
  <a class="seo-guide-btn seo-guide-btn-primary" href="https://workspace.google.com/marketplace/app/create_presentation_with_ai_chatgpt_slid/517982554715">Install Slides by DocGPT.ai</a>
</div>

## One master outline, one deck per segment {#master-deck}

The repeatable version of this workflow is a master outline your whole team reuses:

- **Keep the 8-slide structure fixed** in a shared doc — slots for "what we heard", cost of the status quo, product, rollout, proof, pricing, next steps.
- **Swap only the research block** per prospect or per segment (logistics vs. retail vs. manufacturing), and regenerate.
- **Keep tone constant** so every rep's deck sounds like the same company.
- **Curate a proof library** — real customer outcomes per segment — and paste the relevant one into slide 6 after generating.

Because the output is plain Google Slides, the generated deck slots straight into whatever your team already does with decks: shared drives, comments, brand themes, and PPTX export for the prospect who asks for the file.

## What the AI drafts — and what sales still owns

Slides by DocGPT.ai writes the structure and the slide copy in your chosen tone, grounded in the research you paste. It cannot verify that research, and it does not know your real win stories or current pricing — so proof points, customer names, numbers, and commercial terms need a human pass before the deck is customer-facing. Never paste confidential prospect data you would not put in a normal working document, and treat the generated deck as a strong pre-call draft, not a finished proposal.

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Can AI create a sales deck personalized for each prospect?

Yes. Paste your prospect research — call notes, press, hiring signals, current tooling — into the source-content field, and Slides by DocGPT.ai generates a Google Slides deck framed around that prospect's situation. Keep one master outline and swap the research block per prospect or segment.

### Can the deck match my brand voice?

You choose the writing tone (consultative, direct, formal, friendly, and more) when generating, which keeps copy consistent across the team. Visual branding is then applied the normal Google Slides way: the deck is native and fully editable, so your existing theme, fonts, and logo slides work as usual.

### Can I export the generated deck to PowerPoint?

Yes. The output is a native Google Slides presentation, so you can export it via Google Slides itself with File → Download as PPTX or PDF, share it, or present directly from Slides.


### AI Presentation Maker for Teachers | Slides by DocGPT.ai (https://docgpt.ai/ai-presentation-for-teachers/)

## Short answer {#short-answer}

An **AI presentation maker for teachers** should meet two conditions: the slides must end up somewhere you can freely edit (because no generated deck survives contact with a real class unchanged), and the free tier must cover normal teaching use. [Slides by DocGPT.ai](/google-slide-ai/) does both. It generates a complete Google Slides deck from a topic — you choose the number of slides, the tone, and the language (40+ supported), and you can paste your own lesson plan as source content so the deck follows your structure and timing. The free plan includes 10 AI presentations a month with no credit card, which covers a couple of new lesson decks a week; paid plans start at $14.99/mo if you need more — [see pricing](/google-slide-ai/#pricing).

The path: [install the add-on](https://workspace.google.com/marketplace/app/create_presentation_with_ai_chatgpt_slid/517982554715) → **Extensions → AI GPT for Slides → Start** → topic + lesson plan → generate → fact-check and adapt in Google Slides.

Slides by DocGPT.ai is part of the DocGPT.ai suite used by 500,000+ users.

## How it works {#how-it-works}

1. **Install the add-on** from the [Google Workspace Marketplace](https://workspace.google.com/marketplace/app/create_presentation_with_ai_chatgpt_slid/517982554715). It works inside the Google Slides you already use with Google Classroom and your school's Workspace.
2. **Open the generator**: **Extensions → AI GPT for Slides → Start** in any Google Slides file.
3. **Describe the lesson.** Enter the topic, choose how many slides (around 12 works for a 45-minute lesson), pick a friendly, age-appropriate tone, and select the language.
4. **Paste your lesson plan as source content.** Timings, key concepts, the quiz you want, the homework task — the AI builds the deck around your plan rather than a generic explainer.
5. **Generate, verify, adapt.** The deck is native Google Slides: fix anything the AI got wrong, adjust reading level, add your images and diagrams, and reuse the deck year after year.

Step-by-step guides: [create a presentation from a topic](/docs/slides-ai/create-presentation-from-topic) and [create slides from your own text](/docs/slides-ai/create-slides-from-text).

## Worked example: a 45-minute grade 8 science lesson {#worked-example}

Settings to use in the add-on:

- **Slides:** 12
- **Tone:** friendly and educational
- **Language:** English (or any of 40+)
- **Topic:** `Photosynthesis — 45-minute lesson for grade 8 science`

Paste this into the source-content field, adapted to your own lesson plan:

```
Class: grade 8 science, one 45-minute lesson on photosynthesis.

Build the deck around this lesson plan:
- Starter (5 min): one question slide — "What do plants eat?"
- Core concepts (20 min): chlorophyll and where photosynthesis
  happens; inputs and outputs (carbon dioxide + water + light →
  glucose + oxygen); why it matters for the food chain.
- Worked example (10 min): the word equation, then the balanced
  chemical equation, one slide each.
- Check for understanding (5 min): 3 quiz questions with answers
  on the following slide.
- Recap + homework (5 min): one-slide summary and the homework task:
  label a leaf diagram.

Keep the language at a grade 8 reading level. One idea per slide.
```

The generated deck lands as normal Google Slides: a title slide, the starter question, concept slides with bullets and image+text layouts, the quiz, and the recap. Your editing pass is the professional part — check every factual claim, swap in the diagram your curriculum uses, and tune the quiz to what this class actually struggled with last week.

<div class="seo-guide-inline-cta">
  <div>
    <strong>Build next week's lesson decks in one free plan</strong>
    <span>10 AI presentations a month free — enough for a new deck every couple of teaching days.</span>
  </div>
  <a class="seo-guide-btn seo-guide-btn-primary" href="https://workspace.google.com/marketplace/app/create_presentation_with_ai_chatgpt_slid/517982554715">Install Slides by DocGPT.ai</a>
</div>

## Language classes: generate the deck in the target language {#language-classes}

Because Slides by DocGPT.ai generates in 40+ languages, language teachers get a second workflow for free: write the lesson plan in your language, and generate the deck in the target language. A Spanish teacher can produce an A2-level deck on daily routines *in Spanish*; a French teacher can generate the same cultural topic once in French for the class and once in English for a parents' evening. The language selector applies to the whole deck, and since the output is editable Google Slides, you can adjust vocabulary to exactly the level your class has covered.

## Verify before you teach: the honest note {#honest-limits}

AI-generated educational content must be fact-checked by the teacher — no exceptions. Slides by DocGPT.ai is excellent at structure, pacing, and age-appropriate slide copy, but like any AI it can state facts with confidence that are subtly wrong, outdated, or misaligned with how your curriculum defines a term. Dates, equations, definitions, and quiz answers deserve a line-by-line check before the deck reaches students. The time math still works strongly in your favor: verifying and adapting a complete draft takes a fraction of the time building a deck from a blank slide does — but the verification pass is part of the job, not optional.

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Is there a free AI presentation maker for teachers?

Yes. Slides by DocGPT.ai has a free plan with 10 AI presentations per month and no credit card required — enough for a couple of new lesson decks a week. Paid plans start at $14.99/mo if you need more.

### Can it create lesson slides in other languages?

Yes. You can generate the deck in any of 40+ languages, which is especially useful for language classes: write the lesson plan in your language and generate the slides in the target language, then edit vocabulary to your class's level in Google Slides.

### Can I trust AI-generated facts in lesson slides?

Treat the deck as a draft, not a source of truth. Check dates, definitions, equations, and quiz answers against your curriculum before teaching. The add-on saves the structuring and writing time; the teacher's verification pass is still essential.


### Turn a Blog Post into a Presentation | Slides by DocGPT.ai (https://docgpt.ai/blog-post-to-presentation/)

## Short answer {#short-answer}

**Turning a blog post into a presentation** is a condensing job, not a writing job — the thinking is already done. [Slides by DocGPT.ai](/google-slide-ai/) is built for exactly this: alongside the topic, it accepts your own text as source content, so you paste the full article, choose the number of slides, the tone, and the language (40+ supported), and the AI restructures your post into a deck. The output is a native, fully editable Google Slides presentation — title slide, bullet slides, image+text layouts — so the polish pass happens in the tool your team already uses, and export to PowerPoint or PDF is one **File → Download** away.

The path: [install the add-on](https://workspace.google.com/marketplace/app/create_presentation_with_ai_chatgpt_slid/517982554715) → **Extensions → AI GPT for Slides → Start** → name the deck → paste the post → generate → tighten. Free plan: 10 AI presentations a month, no credit card; paid from $14.99/mo — [see pricing](/google-slide-ai/#pricing).

Slides by DocGPT.ai is part of the DocGPT.ai suite used by 500,000+ users.

## How it works {#how-it-works}

1. **Install the add-on** from the [Google Workspace Marketplace](https://workspace.google.com/marketplace/app/create_presentation_with_ai_chatgpt_slid/517982554715).
2. **Open the generator** in any Google Slides file: **Extensions → AI GPT for Slides → Start**.
3. **Name the deck**, e.g. "Webinar: How we cut onboarding time in half" — a topic that describes the *talk*, not just the post.
4. **Paste the full blog post** into the source-content field, optionally with instructions on how to map sections to slides (see the worked example below).
5. **Set slide count, tone, and language.** Twelve slides suit a 20–30 minute webinar; pick a tone that matches how you present, which can be punchier than how you write.
6. **Generate and polish.** Rearrange slides, cut what does not work out loud, add screenshots and a speaker-notes pass — all natively in Google Slides.

The docs cover this mode in detail: [create slides from your own text](/docs/slides-ai/create-slides-from-text), plus [create a presentation from a topic](/docs/slides-ai/create-presentation-from-topic) when you want a deck without a source article.

## Worked example: a 12-slide webinar deck from one post {#worked-example}

Settings to use in the add-on:

- **Slides:** 12
- **Tone:** conversational and confident
- **Language:** English (or any of 40+)
- **Topic:** `Webinar deck based on our blog post: "How we cut customer onboarding time in half"`

Paste this into the source-content field, with your real article at the bottom:

```
Turn the article below into a 12-slide webinar deck:

- Slide 1: title + speaker name.
- Slides 2–3: the problem the post opens with — why onboarding
  drags, and what it costs.
- Slides 4–9: one key point per slide; use my subheadings as the
  slide titles and keep my terminology.
- Slide 10: the checklist from the end of the post, as a single
  summary slide.
- Slide 11: key takeaways — three bullets.
- Slide 12: call to action — invite questions and point to the
  newsletter.

[Paste the full text of your blog post here]
```

Because the deck is generated from your pasted text, the slides inherit your terminology, your examples, and your argument order — the AI's job is compression, not invention. The human pass afterwards is quick: a written checklist often needs splitting differently on a slide, a joke that works in prose may need cutting, and the CTA slide should match the specific webinar.

<div class="seo-guide-inline-cta">
  <div>
    <strong>Ship the webinar deck this afternoon</strong>
    <span>Install Slides by DocGPT.ai free, paste your best-performing post, and get a 12-slide draft in minutes.</span>
  </div>
  <a class="seo-guide-btn seo-guide-btn-primary" href="https://workspace.google.com/marketplace/app/create_presentation_with_ai_chatgpt_slid/517982554715">Install Slides by DocGPT.ai</a>
</div>

## Where this fits in a repurposing pipeline {#repurposing-pipeline}

One good article can feed a month of formats, and the deck is usually the highest-leverage one:

- **Webinar deck** — the worked example above; the post's structure becomes the talk track.
- **Conference or meetup talk** — regenerate the same post at 15–20 slides with a more formal tone.
- **Internal enablement** — a how-to post becomes the training deck for the team that has to do the thing.
- **Sales leave-behind** — export the deck as PDF via Google Slides and attach it to follow-up emails.

If you run repurposing at scale, the spreadsheet side of the DocGPT.ai suite pairs well with this: the [content repurposing template for GPT for Sheets](/templates/content-repurposing/) turns one post into platform-specific social copy across rows, while Slides by DocGPT.ai handles the deck format here.

## What to expect — and what to fix by hand

The AI condenses your article faithfully, but slides and prose are different media, and the draft will show it: a paragraph's nuance becomes a blunt bullet, transitions that carried the written argument disappear, and anything time-sensitive in the post (pricing, screenshots, stats) ports over unverified. Plan a 15–30 minute polish pass — reorder for spoken flow, rebuild one or two dense slides, refresh dated facts — before the deck goes in front of an audience. That is still a fraction of the half day it takes to build a webinar deck from scratch.

## Frequently Asked Questions

### How do I turn a blog post into a presentation?

Install Slides by DocGPT.ai, open Extensions → AI GPT for Slides → Start in Google Slides, enter a topic for the deck, and paste the full blog post into the source-content field. Set the slide count, tone, and language, then generate. The article is restructured into an editable Google Slides deck.

### Will the slides use my original wording?

The deck is generated from the text you paste, so it keeps your terminology, examples, and argument order — the AI condenses rather than invents. Expect to polish by hand: bullets compress nuance, and spoken flow sometimes needs a different slide order than written flow.

### Can I export the presentation as PowerPoint or PDF?

Yes. The output is a native Google Slides presentation, so you export it from Google Slides itself with File → Download as PPTX or PDF — or present and share it directly in Slides.


### Best AI Presentation Maker for Google Slides (2026) (https://docgpt.ai/best-ai-presentation-maker-google-slides/)

## Short answer {#short-answer}

The "best AI presentation maker for Google Slides" depends on one question first: **do you want the AI inside Google Slides, or are you willing to build decks on a separate platform and export?**

If you live in Google Slides — your team collaborates there, your decks belong in Drive, your sharing and version history matter — an **add-on** is the right shape of tool: **Slides by DocGPT.ai, Plus AI, MagicSlides, and SlidesAI** all generate native, editable Slides decks. Of those, [Slides by DocGPT.ai](/google-slide-ai/) pairs the most generous stated free tier (10 presentations/month) with a straightforward topic-to-deck workflow, 40+ languages, and paid plans from $14.99/mo.

If you're open to a standalone platform, **Gamma** makes striking card-style web decks and **Beautiful.ai** automates design polish — both export to PPTX that you can then import into Google Slides, but the editing happens on their platform, not in Slides.

> All third-party product names are trademarks of their owners. DocGPT.ai is independent and not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by any company named here. Competitor pricing and features below were checked on official sites in July 2026 where possible; where a current price couldn't be verified on the official site, we say so. Always confirm on each vendor's pricing page before buying.

## Comparison table {#comparison}

| Tool | Works inside Google Slides? | Free tier | Starting price | Languages | Export |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Slides by DocGPT.ai** | Yes — Workspace add-on | 10 presentations/mo | From $14.99/mo | 40+ | Native Google Slides; PPTX/PDF via Slides |
| Plus AI | Yes — add-on (also PowerPoint) | 7-day trial only (1,000 credits) | $10/mo billed annually ($15 monthly) | "Any language" advertised | Native Google Slides / PowerPoint |
| MagicSlides | Yes — add-on | Free to start; limits not listed on pricing page | $29/mo (annual saves 17%) | 100–136+ advertised | Google Slides, PPTX, PDF |
| SlidesAI | Yes — add-on (also PowerPoint) | 12 presentations/yr | $10/mo (Pro) | See their site | Google Slides, PowerPoint |
| Gamma | No — standalone platform | Free plan; AI credits don't refresh | See gamma.app pricing (Plus/Pro tiers) | Multilingual | PPTX, PDF, PNG |
| Tome | Product discontinued (April 2025) | — | — | — | — |
| Beautiful.ai | No — standalone platform | 14-day trial (card required); no free plan | $12/mo billed annually (Pro) | See their site | PPTX, PDF |
| Presentations.ai | No — standalone (web + mobile apps) | Free Starter: 100 AI credits/yr, up to 20 slides | $20/mo billed annually (Pro) | See their site | PPTX, PDF |

## Tool-by-tool verdicts {#verdicts}

### Slides by DocGPT.ai — best if you live in Google Slides

Slides by DocGPT.ai generates a complete deck from a topic or your own pasted text, with slide count, writing tone, and 40+ output languages under your control. Because it's a Workspace add-on, the result is a native, editable Google Slides file in your Drive from the first second — collaboration, comments, sharing, versioning, and PPTX/PDF export all work exactly as Slides always has. The free tier covers 10 presentations a month and paid plans start at $14.99/mo ([pricing](/google-slide-ai/#pricing)). It's part of the DocGPT.ai suite used by 500,000+ users. The honest limitation: it's Google Slides-only — if your organization presents from PowerPoint, look at Plus AI or SlidesAI. [Install it here](https://workspace.google.com/marketplace/app/create_presentation_with_ai_chatgpt_slid/517982554715).

### Plus AI — best template polish among the add-ons

Plus AI runs as an extension in both Google Slides and PowerPoint and has earned a reputation for professional, consistent templates. Its Pro plan ($20/mo billed annually) adds document uploads, prompts beyond 100K characters, and AI image generation; the Team plan adds brand controls and shared libraries. The trade-off is entry cost: there's no permanent free plan listed — just a 7-day trial — with Basic at $10/mo billed annually or $15 month-to-month. Full breakdown: [Plus AI vs Slides by DocGPT.ai](/plus-ai-vs-slides-ai-docgpt/).

### MagicSlides — best for converting existing content into slides

MagicSlides' signature is input breadth: its site advertises converting text, topics, YouTube videos, PDFs, URLs, images, and audio into Google Slides decks, and claims 2.3M+ users. Premium is $29/mo (50 presentations/month, up to 50 slides each); Premium Plus is $34/mo for unlimited presentations. It's free to start without a card, though the pricing page doesn't state current free limits. If your decks usually begin from a YouTube video or a PDF, it's worth a trial. Full breakdown: [MagicSlides vs Slides by DocGPT.ai](/magicslides-vs-slides-ai-docgpt/).

### SlidesAI — cheapest paid add-on entry point

SlidesAI works with both Google Slides and PowerPoint and has the lowest published paid price of the add-ons here: Pro at $10/mo (120 presentations/year, 6,000-character input) and Premium at $20.83/mo billed annually for unlimited presentations. Its free plan is real but thin — 12 presentations per year with a 2,500-character input cap — which suits genuinely occasional users. Full breakdown: [SlidesAI vs Slides by DocGPT.ai](/slidesai-vs-slides-ai-docgpt/).

### Gamma — best for card-style web decks, not a Slides tool

Gamma is a standalone platform, and a genuinely good one: its card-based format produces modern, scroll-friendly decks and websites, and it exports to PPTX, PDF, and PNG. But it does not work inside Google Slides — you create and edit on gamma.app, and free-plan AI credits are one-time (they don't refresh; free exports carry a "Made with Gamma" badge, removed on the Plus tier). Its Plus and Pro tiers add monthly credits and API access — see gamma.app for current prices. Choose Gamma when the web-native deck format is the point; choose an add-on when Slides is. Full breakdown: [Gamma vs Slides by DocGPT.ai](/gamma-app-vs-slides-ai-docgpt/).

### Tome — discontinued

Tome shut down its AI presentation product on April 30, 2025, after announcing a pivot away from presentations; the team moved on to a sales-intelligence product. It's listed here because it still appears in older "best AI presentation tool" roundups — if you're migrating away from it, any of the add-ons above will keep your decks in a format you own. Historical comparison: [Tome vs Slides by DocGPT.ai](/tome-app-vs-slides-ai-docgpt/).

### Beautiful.ai — best design automation on its own platform

Beautiful.ai's smart templates reflow layouts as you add content, which keeps non-designers within professional guardrails better than almost anything else. It's a standalone platform (with PowerPoint import/export and PDF export), not a Slides add-on, and there's no free plan — a 14-day trial that requires a credit card, then Pro at $12/mo billed annually or a $45 single-presentation option. Choose it for design automation; skip it if you need to stay inside Google Slides. Full breakdown: [Beautiful.ai vs Slides by DocGPT.ai](/beautiful-ai-vs-slides-ai-docgpt/).

### Presentations.ai — standalone with a usable free starter

Presentations.ai is a standalone web platform with iOS and Android apps, exporting to PowerPoint and PDF with unbranded sharing links on all plans. Its free Starter tier (100 AI credits/year, decks up to 20 slides) is enough to evaluate it properly; Pro is $20/mo billed annually. Like Gamma and Beautiful.ai, the editing lives on its platform rather than in Google Slides. Full breakdown: [Presentations.ai vs Slides by DocGPT.ai](/presentations-ai-vs-slides-ai-docgpt/).

<div class="seo-guide-inline-cta">
  <div>
    <strong>Start with the tool that meets you where you work</strong>
    <span>If that's Google Slides, generate up to 10 presentations a month free with Slides by DocGPT.ai — no new platform to learn.</span>
  </div>
  <a class="seo-guide-btn seo-guide-btn-primary" href="https://workspace.google.com/marketplace/app/create_presentation_with_ai_chatgpt_slid/517982554715">Install Slides by DocGPT.ai</a>
</div>

## How to choose

1. **Slides-native or platform?** If your decks must live in Google Drive with normal Slides collaboration, pick an add-on (Slides by DocGPT.ai, Plus AI, MagicSlides, SlidesAI). If you're happy to present from a web link, Gamma and Presentations.ai open up.
2. **What do your decks start from?** Topics and your own text → Slides by DocGPT.ai. Long documents → Plus AI Pro. YouTube videos, PDFs, URLs → MagicSlides.
3. **How many decks per month?** Under 10 → Slides by DocGPT.ai's free tier covers you. High volume → compare unlimited tiers (MagicSlides Premium Plus, SlidesAI Premium) against your actual usage.
4. **Test before paying.** Run the same real topic through two or three free tiers/trials and judge the drafts, not the marketing.

## Head-to-head comparisons {#head-to-head}

- [Plus AI vs Slides by DocGPT.ai](/plus-ai-vs-slides-ai-docgpt/)
- [MagicSlides vs Slides by DocGPT.ai](/magicslides-vs-slides-ai-docgpt/)
- [SlidesAI vs Slides by DocGPT.ai](/slidesai-vs-slides-ai-docgpt/)
- [Gamma vs Slides by DocGPT.ai](/gamma-app-vs-slides-ai-docgpt/)
- [Tome vs Slides by DocGPT.ai](/tome-app-vs-slides-ai-docgpt/)
- [Beautiful.ai vs Slides by DocGPT.ai](/beautiful-ai-vs-slides-ai-docgpt/)
- [Presentations.ai vs Slides by DocGPT.ai](/presentations-ai-vs-slides-ai-docgpt/)
- [Slidesgo vs Slides by DocGPT.ai](/slidesgo-vs-slides-ai-docgpt/)

More on our product: [Slides by DocGPT.ai](/google-slide-ai/) · [pricing](/google-slide-ai/#pricing) · [documentation](/docs/slides-ai) · [get started](/docs/slides-ai/get-started)

## Frequently Asked Questions

### What is the best AI presentation maker for Google Slides?

For working directly inside Google Slides, use a Workspace add-on: Slides by DocGPT.ai (10 free presentations/month, from $14.99/mo, 40+ languages), Plus AI (best template polish, from $10/mo billed annually), MagicSlides (best input-format breadth, from $29/mo), or SlidesAI (cheapest paid entry, from $10/mo). Standalone platforms like Gamma and Beautiful.ai make excellent decks but require you to create and edit outside Google Slides.

### Can Gamma or Beautiful.ai create decks inside Google Slides?

No. Gamma, Beautiful.ai, Tome (now discontinued), and Presentations.ai are standalone platforms. They can export PPTX files that Google Slides can import, but generation and editing happen on their own platforms, and imported decks may need layout cleanup. Only add-ons — Slides by DocGPT.ai, Plus AI, MagicSlides, SlidesAI — generate native Google Slides decks.

### Is there a free AI presentation maker for Google Slides?

Yes. Slides by DocGPT.ai includes 10 free presentations per month as a Google Slides add-on. SlidesAI's free plan allows 12 presentations per year, and MagicSlides is free to start (check its site for current limits). Gamma and Presentations.ai also have free tiers, but they work outside Google Slides.

## Grok (xAI) in Google Sheets


### GROK function in Google Sheets — =GROK() reference (https://docgpt.ai/docs/gpt-for-sheets/grok-function)

# =GROK() — use Grok (xAI) in Google Sheets

`=GROK()` runs xAI's Grok models from a cell in Google Sheets. Type a prompt, reference your data, drag the formula down — Grok fills the column. There is no native Grok integration for Google Workspace, so the add-on formula is the practical way to use Grok on spreadsheet data.

## Syntax

```
=GROK(prompt, [value])
```

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `prompt` | yes | The instruction for Grok. |
| `value` | no | Extra input appended to the prompt, usually a cell reference. |

## Requirements

Unlike `=GPT()`, `=CLAUDE()` and `=GEMINI()` (built in, no keys), `=GROK()` uses **your own xAI API key**:

1. A **User Pro or Business plan** of GPT for Sheets — bring-your-own-key functions are a paid feature.
2. An **xAI API key** — see [how to get a Grok API key](/grok-api-key-google-sheets-setup/).

Add the key via **Extensions → GPT for Sheets → Set Up API Key → Grok (xAI)**. Requests then go to xAI on your key, billed by xAI at their API rates.

## Model options

Pick the model in the same Set Up API Key dialog:

| Model | When to use |
|---|---|
| `grok-4.3` | Newest flagship — hardest reasoning and writing tasks. |
| `grok-4-latest` | Best current general option. |
| `grok-3` (default) | Legacy default; stable for everyday tasks. |
| `grok-3-mini` / `grok-3-fast` / `grok-3-mini-fast` | Cheaper and faster legacy options for simple bulk transformations. |

## Examples

Research a company in `A2`:

```
=GROK("In 20 words, what does this company do and who are its customers?", A2)
```

Classify support tickets in `A2`:

```
=GROK("Classify as Billing, Bug, Feature request or Other. Return one word.", A2)
```

Draft a reply to a review in `A2`:

```
=GROK("Write a two-sentence reply to this customer review, friendly and specific", A2)
```

Score a lead row (company in `A2`, title in `B2`):

```
=GROK("Score 1-10 how well this fits an ICP of B2B SaaS marketing teams. Return the number, then a 10-word reason.", A2&" — "&B2)
```

## Tips

- Grok error in the cell usually means the key is missing or invalid — re-check **Set Up API Key**; the formula tells you which step failed.
- Costs are controlled by xAI's API pricing on your key, so `grok-3-mini` variants can make large bulk runs significantly cheaper.
- Drag down for bulk runs, then use **Replace all GPT formulas with results** in the sidebar to freeze the output and stop re-computation.
- Prefer no API keys at all? `=GPT()`, `=CLAUDE()`, `=GEMINI()`, `=MISTRAL()` and `=PERPLEXITY()` run without any key on all plans.

## Related functions

- <a href="/docs/gpt-for-sheets/deepseek-function">=DEEPSEEK()</a> — another bring-your-own-key model function
- <a href="/docs/gpt-for-sheets/gpt-function">=GPT()</a> — built-in default, no key required
- <a href="/docs/gpt-for-sheets/perplexity-function">=PERPLEXITY()</a> — built-in web-aware research

## Try it

<a href="https://workspace.google.com/marketplace/app/gpt_for_sheets_docs_forms_slides/466607203252">Install GPT for Sheets</a>. Guides: <a href="/grok-for-google-sheets-how-to-guide/">connect Grok to Google Sheets</a>, <a href="/grok-for-google-sheets-lead-enrichment/">Grok lead-enrichment formulas</a>, <a href="/grok-edit-google-sheets-ai/">can Grok edit Google Sheets?</a>


### DEEPSEEK function in Google Sheets — =DEEPSEEK() reference (https://docgpt.ai/docs/gpt-for-sheets/deepseek-function)

# =DEEPSEEK() — use DeepSeek in Google Sheets

`=DEEPSEEK()` runs DeepSeek models from a cell in Google Sheets. DeepSeek's API pricing is among the lowest of any capable model, which makes it attractive for very large bulk runs on your own key.

## Syntax

```
=DEEPSEEK(prompt, [value])
```

| Parameter | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `prompt` | yes | The instruction for DeepSeek. |
| `value` | no | Extra input appended to the prompt, usually a cell reference. |

## Requirements

Like `=GROK()`, this is a bring-your-own-key function:

1. A **User Pro or Business plan** of GPT for Sheets.
2. A **DeepSeek API key** from platform.deepseek.com.

Add it via **Extensions → GPT for Sheets → Set Up API Key → DeepSeek**. Requests are billed by DeepSeek on your key.

## Model options

| Model | When to use |
|---|---|
| `deepseek-chat` (default) | Writing, extraction, classification and everyday spreadsheet text tasks. |
| `deepseek-reasoner` | Logic, math, planning and multi-step prompts — slower but more careful. |

## Examples

Summarize feedback in `A2`:

```
=DEEPSEEK("Summarize in one sentence, keep the customer's main complaint", A2)
```

Extract fields from messy text in `A2`:

```
=DEEPSEEK("Extract the email address. Return only the email or 'none'.", A2)
```

Bulk-translate descriptions in `A2`:

```
=DEEPSEEK("Translate to German, return only the translation", A2)
```

## Tips

- `deepseek-chat` covers most spreadsheet tasks; switch to `deepseek-reasoner` only when rows need multi-step logic — it is slower.
- For bulk runs, freeze results with **Replace all GPT formulas with results** when done.
- No key and no paid plan? The built-in `=GPT()`, `=CLAUDE()`, `=GEMINI()` and `=MISTRAL()` need no configuration.

## Related functions

- <a href="/docs/gpt-for-sheets/grok-function">=GROK()</a> — the other bring-your-own-key model function
- <a href="/docs/gpt-for-sheets/mistral-function">=MISTRAL()</a> — built-in fast, low-cost option
- <a href="/docs/gpt-for-sheets/gpt-function">=GPT()</a> — built-in default

## Try it

<a href="https://workspace.google.com/marketplace/app/gpt_for_sheets_docs_forms_slides/466607203252">Install GPT for Sheets</a>. See also: <a href="/can-deepseek-edit-google-sheets-ai/">can DeepSeek edit Google Sheets?</a>


### Grok for Google Workspace — What Works Today (https://docgpt.ai/grok-for-google-workspace/)

**Short answer:** xAI ships no Grok connector for Google Workspace. If you searched for one, what you actually can do today is run Grok **inside Google Sheets** with the [GPT for Sheets](/gpt-for-sheets/) add-on: a `=GROK()` formula that calls xAI on your own API key, row by row, at bulk speed.

## What works today

| Where | Grok support | What to use |
|---|---|---|
| **Google Sheets** | ✅ `=GROK()` formula via GPT for Sheets (your xAI key) | [=GROK() reference](/docs/gpt-for-sheets/grok-function) |
| **Google Docs** | ❌ no Grok; ChatGPT/Claude/Gemini available | <a href="/google-doc-ai/">GPT for Docs</a> |
| **Gmail** | ❌ no Grok; AI drafting available | <a href="/ai-email-assistant/">AI Email Assistant</a> |
| **Google Slides** | ❌ no Grok; AI deck generation available | <a href="/google-slide-ai/">Slides by DocGPT.ai</a> |

## Set up Grok in Google Sheets (3 steps)

1. Install <a href="https://workspace.google.com/marketplace/app/gpt_for_sheets_docs_forms_slides/466607203252">GPT for Sheets</a> from the Google Workspace Marketplace.
2. Open **Extensions → GPT for Sheets → Set Up API Key**, choose **Grok (xAI)**, paste your key and pick a model (`grok-4-latest` for quality, `grok-3-mini` for cheap bulk runs). Getting a key takes ~2 minutes: [step-by-step guide](/grok-api-key-google-sheets-setup/).
3. In any cell:

```
=GROK("In 15 words, what does this company do?", A2)
```

Drag down — every row gets its own Grok answer. When the run finishes, use **Replace all GPT formulas with results** to freeze the output.

Bring-your-own-key functions (`=GROK()`, `=DEEPSEEK()`) require the **User Pro or Business plan** — see <a href="/gpt-for-sheets/#pricing">pricing</a>. API usage is billed separately by xAI on your key.

## What people use Grok in Sheets for

- **Lead research and enrichment** — company summaries, industry tags, ICP fit scores: [Grok lead-enrichment formulas](/grok-for-google-sheets-lead-enrichment/)
- **Bulk classification** — tickets, reviews, keywords into fixed categories
- **Content drafting** — outreach openers, replies, short descriptions across hundreds of rows
- **Data cleanup** — extract fields, normalize names and categories without regex

## Honest limits

- `=GROK()` covers Google Sheets only. No add-on currently puts Grok itself into Docs, Gmail or Slides — those apps get ChatGPT, Claude and Gemini through the other DocGPT.ai add-ons.
- Grok answers come from the model, not from a live crawl of your Workspace — it sees exactly the cell data you pass it.
- Large runs cost money on your xAI key; test on 10 rows first and prefer the mini models for simple transformations.

## FAQ

### Does Grok integrate with Google Workspace?

No — xAI offers no native Workspace integration. The practical route is the `=GROK()` formula in Google Sheets via GPT for Sheets, on your own xAI API key.

### How do I connect Grok to Google Sheets?

Install GPT for Sheets, add your xAI key under **Set Up API Key**, pick a model, then use `=GROK("prompt", A2)` and drag down. Requires the User Pro or Business plan.

### Can Grok read or edit Google Docs and Gmail?

Not natively. For AI inside Docs and Gmail, use GPT for Docs and AI Email Assistant (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini). In Sheets, `=GROK()` gives you Grok itself.
